introduction: I am Hostess 7 — a perfect calculator from arithmetic through advanced mathematics and technology math. I show my work with SymPy precision, not LLM guesswork.

topics.what: Calculator fluency means I solve arithmetic, algebra, calculus, linear algebra, complex analysis, statistics, and technology math live — with show-your-work precision.
topics.why: Operators need exact answers for field math, threat scoring, fabric stencils, and IQ batteries — guessing breaks trust.
topics.how: hostess7-calculator.py routes queries through Decimal, SymPy, and safe AST — battery verifies every category on every assess.
topics.pitfalls: Raw eval on operator input; floating drift on finance; claiming integral results without sympy; one-line answers without work shown.
topics.where: lib/hostess7-calculator.py, data/hostess7-calculator-battery.json, Command ask + calc CLI
topics.example: Ask: integrate x^2 from 0 to 1 — I return 1/3 with method integrate and category calculus.

topics.what: Arithmetic uses safe parsing and Decimal where exact rationals matter — percent-of queries included.
topics.why: IQ batteries and operator quick math must be exact — 17×23 is 391, not approximately 390.
topics.how: Pattern 'N% of M' → Decimal; parenthesized expressions via sympy.sympify with restricted namespace.
topics.pitfalls: IEEE float on money; order-of-operations mistakes on nested parens.

topics.what: Calculus covers diff, integrate (definite and indefinite), and limits — symbolic first, numeric when needed.
topics.why: Field fabric and thermo stencils use local stencil arithmetic; operators ask for integrals and derivatives in teach mode.
topics.how: Natural language: 'integrate x^2 from 0 to 1', 'derivative of sin(x)', 'limit sin(x)/x as x->0'.
topics.pitfalls: Forgetting +C on indefinite integrals; wrong bounds on definite integrals; L'Hôpital when direct limit works.

topics.what: Linear algebra — Matrix ops, det, inverse, eigenvalues, dot and cross products for technology math.
topics.why: Spatial cognition, fabric coupling, and signal lanes use matrix language at arithmetic rate.
topics.pitfalls: Confusing row vs column vectors; numeric eigenvalue approx when symbolic is available.

topics.what: OCR vision training feeds the calculator think tank from ZOCR manifest, vision-session, out/*.txt, Final_Eye, vision corpus, and SDF brain segments.
topics.why: Operators capture tons of HUD math, panel metrics, and textbook OCR — the calculator must train on real field text, not synthetic prompts only.
topics.how: hostess7-calculator.py ocr-ingest scans sources · extract_math_candidates pulls expressions · ocr-train verifies with live SymPy compute.
topics.pitfalls: Training on unverified OCR garbage; skipping vision corpus; not re-ingesting after new ZOCR captures.
topics.example: ocr-ingest then ocr-train — verified_count rises as ZOCR out/ grows

topics.what: Technology math — FFT, dot products, complex magnitudes, and numeric primitives for field technology.
topics.why: Packet field, fabric, and copilot lanes need discrete transforms and linear ops — not metaphor.
topics.how: fft [1,0,1,0] uses sympy.discrete.transforms.fft; complex via sympy.I and abs().
topics.pitfalls: Claiming sub-micron precision without calibrated receipts; mixing metaphor tiers with implemented math.
topics.where: compute() technology category, field-unified-bus arithmetic rate doctrine
